articles.title: MERCEDES-BENZ UNVEILS FIRST BRANDED RESIDENTIAL SKYSCRAPER IN DUBAI
articles.summary: Mercedes-Benz and Binghatti have revealed plans for a residential skyscraper that will be 341 meters high. The first branded renders show a sinuous tower with a curved pinnacle and facades imprinted with the brand's three-pointed star. The German carmaker is one of many in the industry developing residential properties. Bentley, Aston Martin, and Porsche are also doing it.

articles.title: WE JUST GOT OUR FIRST GLIMPSE AT APPLE'S OFFICIAL VISION PRO CASE DESIGN
articles.summary: Apple's upcoming Vision Pro headset is set to launch soon. A newly discovered patent suggests that Apple may release a fabric cover for the front of the headset as a protective accessory. This cover is reminiscent of the charging cover for Apple's AirPods Max and has sparked humorous comparisons to certain types of underwear due to the headset's design.

articles.title: REBIRTH OF THE UX UNICORN?
articles.summary: UX professionals who are good at all UX lifecycle steps, including research and design, are called “UX unicorns” because they are rare. However, AI narrows skill gaps and may create many more UX unicorns.

articles.title: HERE'S WHY YOU REALLY NEED THE LATEST M2 IPAD PRO IF YOU'RE A CREATIVE
articles.summary: The M2 iPad Pro is proving to be a powerful and versatile tool for creative users. Despite initial disappointment during unboxing due to its physical resemblance to the previous model, the M2 iPad Pro has impressed users with its seamless multitasking capabilities and its ability to handle various creative tasks effortlessly.

articles.title: DITCH ‘UX’, IT’S TIME FOR PRODUCT EXPERIENCES
articles.summary: A lot can happen between perfect user testing sessions and the live experience. When you think about product experiences instead of user experiences, you get better results, since you consider all the technical factors influencing user engagement. Embracing the full scope of product design mitigates a crucial weakness for many designers: collaborating with business and engineering.

articles.title: REAL-TIME AI DRAWING
articles.summary: Turn iPad sketches into vector illustrations in real time.

articles.title: EASING MADE EASY
articles.summary: Quickly and easily generate easing curves for ease, spring, and bounce animations.

articles.title: RESPONSIVE WEBSITE FRAMER TEMPLATES
articles.summary: This collection features over 1,000 Framer templates with easily customized images, content, and style.

articles.title: THE DAWN OF THE TECH GENERALIST RENAISSANCE
articles.summary: The internet gave digital designers and programmers incredible leverage to create and distribute their ideas far and wide for little cost. Now, with AI, a single savvy designer or programmer can create what used to take a team of highly skilled experts.

articles.title: HOW INCLUSIVE DESIGN SHAPED ME
articles.summary: Disabled people should be able to highlight their particular needs without fearing exclusion from working life. A designer with disabilities openly shares how their disabilities, and consequently inclusive design, shaped them.

articles.title: THE ART OF THE PROFESSIONAL PIVOT
articles.summary: Six creatives share how relocating helped them refine skills, explore deeper interests, and better match passions with careers in product development. Each interviewee used a different pivot technique, ranging from the let’s-blow-this-thing-up-hard-left to a simple reframe. This post covers each in detail so you can plan your next big move.

articles.title: TELL BETTER STORIES
articles.summary: 9 principles that designers can use to tell better stories. The principles were inspired by Dieter Rams' 10 Principles of Good Design and Ted Sorensen's approach to writing speeches for President John F. Kennedy.

articles.title: GRAPHIC DESIGN HARMONY: THE GOLDEN RATIO’S INFLUENCE
articles.summary: Using the golden ratio, you can create perfectly balanced layouts and compositions based on natural patterns. This post features various layouts created using it, such as logos, typography, packaging, and more.

articles.title: UI/UX DESIGN TRENDS FOR 2024
articles.summary: There's a lot to look forward to in 2024. Explore the cutting-edge technologies, styles, and tools that will redefine the digital experience in the new year and related critical growth opportunities.
